logline: Two British soldiers are given an impossible mission: cross enemy territory to deliver a message that will stop 1,600 men from walking into a deadly trap.
antagonistOrConflict: Time, distance, and the German army—Schofield must traverse No Man's Land and enemy territory before dawn to prevent a massacre
tone: Relentless, immersive, visceral—designed to feel like real-time experience with moments of haunting beauty amid horror
themes: The cost of duty and sacrifice
The randomness of survival in war
The individual versus the machinery of war

actBreaks.description: Blake is fatally stabbed by the German pilot they rescued, transforming Schofield's mission from shared duty to personal obligation

validation.ruling: Scene #25 (EXT. REAR FRENCH FARMHOUSE) is the irreversible turning point where the German pilot stabs and kills Blake—this fundamentally transforms the story from a two-man mission into Schofield's solo journey, raising stakes exponentially and forcing the protagonist into entirely new emotional and physical territory from which the story cannot return.

actBreaks.description: Schofield emerges from the river into dawn and discovers British troops, shifting from solitary survival to the final race against time

validation.ruling: Scene #50 (EXT. PINE WOOD) marks Schofield's spiritual rebirth after his near-death in the river—encountering the company singing creates a thematic shift from despair to renewed purpose, functioning as the final transition before the climactic assault on the trenches, though this is a quieter, more introspective act break than traditional structure.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script operates as a ticking-clock thriller disguised as a war film, structured around a single continuous journey that feels almost real-time. Act One establishes the mission and partnership, ending with Blake's death at scene 25—the emotional gut-punch that redefines everything. Act Two becomes Schofield's solo odyssey through hell: the truck convoy, the sniper at the canal, the nightmarish chase through Ecoust, the river plunge. It's pure survival mode, stripped of dialogue, driven by environmental obstacles. Act Three begins at scene 50 when Schofield finds British troops in the pine wood—suddenly he's no longer alone, and the mission becomes achievable but desperately urgent. The structure is deceptively simple: get from Point A to Point B. But the genius is in how each act reframes the stakes—from duty, to grief-fueled determination, to frantic race against the clock.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing is relentless but rhythmic, with brief breathing spaces that make the tension sustainable. Scenes 1-7 move quickly through trenches (2.2 pages average), establishing urgency. Scene 8 (No Man's Land) expands to 5 pages, the first major set piece. The German dugout sequence (scenes 12-13) creates claustrophobic dread. Blake's death (scene 25) is the emotional brake that forces a reset. The Ecoust sequence (scenes 34-48) is a masterclass in sustained chase—15 rapid-fire scenes averaging 1 page each, pure kinetic energy. The river (scene 48) provides the script's only true pause—a moment of near-death that transitions into the ethereal pine wood scene. The final push (scenes 51-58) accelerates again, intercutting Schofield's desperate run with the attack already underway.

incitingIncident.description: General Erinmore reveals Blake's brother is with the 2nd Devons, who are walking into a German trap; he orders Blake and Schofield to cross No Man's Land and deliver a message to stop the attack by dawn or sixteen hundred men will die.

validation.ruling: Scene #4 (INT. DUGOUT) shatters the ordinary world when General Erinmore reveals Blake's brother is in mortal danger and assigns the mission to stop an attack that will kill 1600 men, establishing the irreversible stakes and main conflict that forces both protagonists into action.

midpoint.description: The convoy stops at a canal with no bridge for six miles; Captain Smith warns Schofield to make sure there are witnesses when he delivers the message to Colonel Mackenzie, implying the Colonel may not want to call off the attack.

validation.ruling: Scene #30 is a transitional moment of reflection, not a true midpoint shift. Scene #31 (EXT. CANAL - CONTINUOUS) is the point of no return where Schofield must swim across the canal under sniper fire—now alone after Blake's death, he shifts from reactive survival to proactive determination, raising stakes as he becomes the sole bearer of the mission with no possibility of retreat.

climax.description: Schofield bursts into Mackenzie's dugout and hands the Colonel General Erinmore's letter ordering the attack to stop, which Mackenzie reads before ordering the attack called off.

validation.ruling: Scene #58 (INT. MACKENZIE'S DUGOUT) is the ultimate confrontation where Schofield directly faces Colonel Mackenzie to stop the attack, resolving the main story question through intense verbal conflict as Mackenzie resists the orders—this is the peak dramatic tension where the mission's success or failure is determined, demonstrating Schofield's completed arc as he fights to honor Blake's sacrifice.

scenes.heading: EXT. MEADOW - DAY, APRIL 6TH 1917
scenes.summary: Schofield and Blake rest in a meadow when Sergeant Sanders wakes Blake and orders him to pick a man and bring his kit; Blake chooses Schofield and they prepare to move.

scenes.heading: EXT. COMMS TRENCH - DAY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake and Schofield walk through a narrow communications trench, sharing stale bread and discussing Blake's cancelled leave; Schofield suggests it's easier not to go home at all.

scenes.heading: EXT. SECOND TRENCH - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Sanders leads Blake and Schofield through a wider trench where Blake asks about the big push; Sanders sarcastically deflects and they navigate around Royal Engineers working on telegraph wires.

scenes.heading: INT. DUGOUT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: General Erinmore reveals Blake's brother is with the 2nd Devons, who are walking into a German trap; he orders Blake and Schofield to cross No Man's Land and deliver a message to stop the attack by dawn or sixteen hundred men will die.
scenes.turningPointNote: The mission is revealed — Schofield and Blake must prevent a massacre by crossing enemy territory before dawn

scenes.heading: EXT. SECOND LINE TRENCH - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Schofield tries to convince Blake to wait until dark before crossing No Man's Land, but Blake insists on leaving immediately because his brother's life is at stake and refuses to delay.

scenes.heading: EXT. SAUCHIEHALL LINE (COMMS “DOWN” TRENCH)- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake pushes through a narrow communications trench against traffic flow while Schofield follows, arguing they have time to wait for sunset; Blake refuses to slow down.

scenes.heading: EXT. PARADISE (FRONT LINE) TRENCH - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake and Schofield reach the front line trench called Paradise Alley and search for the Yorks; they navigate through the eerie, crowded trench past wounded soldiers and dire warning signs, with Schofield growing increasingly fearful.

scenes.heading: EXT. NO MAN'S LAND - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake and Schofield climb out of the trench into No Man's Land, crawling through British wire and past dead horses covered in flies; Schofield cuts his hand on German wire as they navigate the treacherous, empty landscape toward the German trenches.

scenes.heading: EXT. GERMAN FRONT LINE TRENCH - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake and Schofield drop into the empty German front line trench; Schofield pours water on his bleeding hand while Blake jokes about it, and they discover the trench is blocked by debris.

scenes.heading: EXT. GERMAN COMMS TRENCH - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake leads them quickly through a deep, narrow German communications trench, checking for enemies as they move forward.

scenes.heading: EXT. GERMAN SECOND LINE TRENCH - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake and Schofield find the German second line trench also blocked by a direct hit; they move cautiously with bayonets ready, discovering the trench is destroyed and impassable.

scenes.heading: INT. GERMAN DUGOUT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake discovers a massive German dugout with rows of bunk beds and palatial quarters; Schofield finds a tunnel leading through while Blake sits in an officer's chair and discovers a bottle of wine, which he opens and drinks.

scenes.heading: INT. GERMAN TUNNEL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake and Schofield navigate a chalk tunnel that groans and collapses around them; Blake nearly falls into a mineshaft but stops in time, then decides they must jump across it.

scenes.heading: EXT. REAR GERMAN TRENCHES - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake and Schofield emerge from the tunnel into a massive abandoned quarry filled with damaged German guns and spent shell casings; Schofield struggles to catch his breath and clean dust from his eyes while Blake surveys the desolate area.

scenes.heading: EXT. SHATTERED COPSE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake tells Schofield a story about a soldier named Wilko who put oil in his hair and woke to find a rat licking it, which bit through his ear; they laugh together as they walk through a burnt copse.

scenes.heading: EXT. WALLED ORCHARD - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake and Schofield enter a walled orchard where all the cherry trees have been cut down; Blake picks a blossom and identifies the trees as Lamberts or Dukes, explaining the difference between cherry varieties as they walk through the destruction.

scenes.heading: INT. FRENCH FARMHOUSE, HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Schofield enters a French farmhouse hallway, listening for sounds of life in the silent, trashed building before moving into a bedroom.

scenes.heading: INT. FRENCH FARMHOUSE, BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Schofield moves through an empty bedroom and returns to the hallway.

scenes.heading: INT. FRENCH FARMHOUSE, HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Schofield spots Blake through a window and tells him he found nothing; he notices a child's doll with cigarette burns on its eyes before moving toward the kitchen.

scenes.heading: INT. FRENCH FARMHOUSE, KITCHEN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake enters the kitchen through the back door asking about food; Schofield says he found nothing and tells Blake he doesn't like this place before exiting.

scenes.heading: EXT. REAR FRENCH FARMHOUSE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Schofield moves across the barren yard toward a dilapidated barn.

scenes.heading: INT. BARN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Schofield finds a bucket of milk in the barn and tastes it; Blake exits the farmhouse and tells him the map shows they need to cross a ridge for a straight run to Ecoust.

scenes.heading: EXT. REAR FRENCH FARMHOUSE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Schofield watches three planes engaged in a dogfight high in the sky—two British fighters against one German; Blake joins him and they observe the aerial combat, with Schofield believing the British are winning.

scenes.heading: INT. BARN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The German plane crashes into the barn with the pilot screaming inside; Blake and Schofield rush in and pull the burning pilot from the wreckage, with Blake burning his hand on the hot metal.

scenes.heading: EXT. REAR FRENCH FARMHOUSE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Blake and Schofield drag the severely burned German pilot outside; Blake cradles him and asks Schofield to get water, but as Schofield turns away, the pilot stabs Blake in the stomach with a knife; Schofield shoots the pilot dead and tries desperately to save Blake, who dies asking Schofield to write to his mother and tell her he wasn't scared.
scenes.turningPointNote: Blake is killed by the German pilot he tried to save — Schofield must now complete the mission alone

scenes.heading: EXT. COUNTRY ROAD - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Schofield encounters a convoy of trucks on a country road where Colonel Collins and soldiers are trying to move a fallen tree; Captain Smith offers Schofield a ride after learning about his mission to stop the attack.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMY TRUCK - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Schofield rides in a crowded army truck with twenty soldiers from various companies; he tells them he must reach the 2nd Devons to stop an attack at dawn because sixteen hundred men are walking into a trap, and that his partner was killed.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROADSIDE DITCH - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The truck gets stuck in a muddy ditch; Schofield instructs the driver to reverse and orders everyone out to push, but the truck remains stuck until they use planks for traction and successfully free it.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMY TRUCK - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The soldiers in the truck discuss Schofield's mission and express doubt he'll make it; Schofield insists he will, accepts whisky from Butler, and they all watch as flares light up the sky ahead, revealing they're approaching the front.

scenes.heading: EXT. CANAL SIDE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The convoy stops at a canal with no bridge for six miles; Captain Smith warns Schofield to make sure there are witnesses when he delivers the message to Colonel Mackenzie, implying the Colonel may not want to call off the attack.
scenes.turningPointNote: Smith warns Schofield that Mackenzie may resist the order — the mission becomes more complex than simple delivery

scenes.heading: EXT. CANAL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Schofield swims across the canal under sniper fire from a German shooter in the lock house; he takes cover against the far bank, identifies the shooter's position in an upper window, and prepares his rifle with shaking hands before advancing up stairs toward the building.

scenes.heading: INT. LOCK HOUSE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Schofield enters the lock house and cautiously climbs the stairs with his rifle ready; he pushes open the upper room door to find a German soldier slumped dead against the wall.

scenes.heading: INT. LOCK HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Schofield wakes in darkness at the bottom of the stairs with a head wound and blood on his hands; he realizes it's now night and struggles to orient himself, discovering his watch is broken.

scenes.heading: EXT. ECOUST, CANAL SIDE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Schofield staggers through the destroyed town of Ecoust in darkness as flares illuminate the landscape; he struggles to distinguish shadows from reality in the undulating, war-torn environment.

scenes.heading: EXT. ALLEY WAY, ECOUST - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Schofield moves through a narrow, dark alleyway toward flickering light at the end.

scenes.heading: EXT. MAIN STREET, ECOUST - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Schofield emerges onto a destroyed main street and cautiously walks toward the main square, where a large fire burns beyond a colonnade; he moves warily through the empty, shelled buildings.

scenes.heading: EXT. ECOUST MAIN SQUARE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Schofield enters the devastated main square where the church is on fire; he spots a man's silhouette in front of the flames and realizes he's been seen.

scenes.heading: EXT. SIDE STREET, ECOUST - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Schofield runs from a German soldier chasing him; he finds a locked cellar window, then scrambles through a coal chute to escape as the soldier's footsteps approach.

scenes.heading: INT. COAL CELLAR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Schofield hides in a coal cellar as German boots run past; he discovers a makeshift living space with a fire and finds a young French woman named Lauri hiding with a baby; she reveals she's been hiding for weeks, and Schofield gives her his food and water before she directs him toward the British lines.

scenes.heading: INT. HOUSE, ECOUST - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Schofield climbs damaged stairs and peers out at the empty street from inside the house.

scenes.heading: EXT. SIDE STREET, ECOUST - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Schofield moves through shadows along the street in the direction Lauri indicated; he reaches a crossroads and turns into an alley where a drunk German soldier stumbles out of a door and vomits.

scenes.heading: INT. SCHOOL HOUSE, ECOUST - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Schofield enters a destroyed school assembly hall and hides in shadows; another German soldier emerges from the darkness, and they lock eyes before Schofield shoots him dead and flees.

scenes.heading: EXT. SIDE STREET, ECOUST - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Schofield runs through the street as the drunk German soldier Muller chases him shouting and shooting; bullets strike the wall near Schofield as he flees.

scenes.heading: EXT. SMALL STREET, ECOUST - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Schofield sprints and encounters another German soldier ahead who begins shooting; Schofield breaks left across the street to escape.

scenes.heading: EXT. TINY STREET, ECOUST - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Schofield runs down a narrow alley as Muller and the other German chase him firing shots; he turns a corner toward stone steps.

scenes.heading: EXT. CURVED STAIRS STREET, ECOUST - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Schofield leaps down stone steps three or four at a time and runs toward a bridge at the end of a long straight street.

scenes.heading: EXT. BRIDGE STREET, ECOUST - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Schofield sprints downhill toward the bridge as the two Germans fire at him; bullets strike the cobbles around him, and as he reaches the bridge under heavy fire, he suddenly vaults over the stone wall into the river below.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVER, ECOUST - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Schofield plunges into the river and struggles to stay afloat, shedding his webbing; the rapids sweep him downstream past the Germans, through dangerous rocks and a waterfall, until he finally grabs onto a fallen tree and pulls himself to the riverbank.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVER BANK - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Schofield collapses on the riverbank and cries for Blake, the baby, and everything he's endured; as morning forms, he hears distant singing and walks toward it through the woods.

scenes.heading: EXT. PINE WOOD - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: EXT. 2ND COMMS TRENCH - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Schofield runs through the comms trench, shoving past advancing soldiers and demanding to know where the commanding officer is located.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOLDING PEN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Schofield pushes through the packed holding area to reach Lieutenant Hutton and tells him the attack has been called off by General Erinmore.

scenes.heading: EXT. 2ND FRONT LINE TRENCH - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Schofield pushes through the crowded front line trench to reach Captain Ivins, who is crying and terrified, and tries to tell him the attack is called off before German artillery begins shelling overhead.

scenes.heading: EXT. NO MAN'S LAND - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Schofield runs across No Man's Land parallel to the trench as six hundred soldiers pour out behind him toward the German lines, with men falling by the dozens as German guns erupt.

scenes.heading: EXT. 2ND COMMAND TRENCH - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Schofield tumbles into the command trench among B Company soldiers, asks Captain Sandbach for Colonel Mackenzie's location, and is directed to a dugout.

scenes.heading: INT. MACKENZIE’S DUGOUT ANTE-ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Two orderlies physically restrain Schofield and haul him out of the dugout ante-room as he tries to reach Colonel Mackenzie.

scenes.heading: EXT. 2ND COMMAND TRENCH - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Schofield struggles against the orderlies pinning him to the trench wall as Captain Rylands orders the next wave to be sent, prompting Schofield to break free and rush into the dugout.

scenes.heading: INT. MACKENZIE'S DUGOUT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Schofield bursts into Mackenzie's dugout and hands the Colonel General Erinmore's letter ordering the attack to stop, which Mackenzie reads before ordering the attack called off.
scenes.turningPointNote: Schofield successfully delivers the message to Colonel Mackenzie, who calls off the attack—the mission's objective is achieved.

scenes.heading: EXT. 2ND TRENCH - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Schofield walks along the trench past wounded and dying men from B, C, and D Companies as survivors are gathered and carried, searching for Lieutenant Blake.

scenes.heading: EXT. SHATTERED COMMS TRENCH - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Schofield pushes through the comms trench past stretcher bearers, asks a sergeant about Lieutenant Blake's location, and receives no answer before following them up a slope.

scenes.heading: EXT. MEADOW - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Schofield searches through the field station tents asking for Lieutenant Blake, finds no officers matching the description among the wounded, then hears an officer's voice and turns to see a man whose similarity to Blake's brother catches his attention.
scenes.turningPointNote: Schofield locates Lieutenant Blake, completing his secondary mission to deliver news of his brother's death.
